headline: Sabah-Philippines Crime Ring Busted With 45 Arrests
context: Joint operation dismantled 45-member cross-border crime syndicate operating in Sandakan and Zamboanga in March 2026.

cards.big: 45-member cross-border crime ring dismantled in Sabah-Philippines operation
cards.sub: Syndicate operated kidnapping smuggling and document forgery since 2021

cards.big: Group facilitated entry of 8,000 undocumented immigrants over 4 years
cards.sub: Each illegal entry charged RM2,000-5,000 generating RM28 million revenue

cards.big: 12 Malaysian immigration officers arrested for complicity in facilitation
cards.sub: Officers allegedly received monthly payments of RM3,000-8,000 from syndicate

cards.big: Joint operation involved 320 personnel from PDRM ESSCOM and Philippine police
cards.sub: Intelligence sharing agreement signed 2024 enabled 6 months coordinated surveillance

cards.big: Cross-border busts test whether bilateral cooperation can dismantle entrenched networks
45 arrests in the Sabah-Philippines crime ring expose the security cost of an unresolved border.
